content.content:
content: '# Web Document


  ## Metadata


  - title: MyChart Appointment and Scheduling Troubleshooting | Cleveland Clinic

  - url: https://my.clevelandclinic.org/resources/mychart-appointment-and-scheduling-troubleshooting

  - source: Cleveland Clinic

  - domain: my.clevelandclinic.org


  ## Content


  MyChart Appointment and Scheduling Troubleshooting


  Cleveland Clinic explains that patients can manage, book, request and cancel appointments
  in MyChart. Patients can also check in when they arrive for an appointment.


  Scheduling Features


  MyChart can be used to book appointments directly with providers, request appointments
  if direct scheduling is not available, and use ticket scheduling or open scheduling
  when applicable. If insurance is registered in the patient''s MyChart account, the
  patient can schedule or request appointments with participating providers. If a
  patient is self-pay or does not have coverage, Cleveland Clinic says the patient
  needs to meet with a Patient Financial Advocate before scheduling.


  Direct scheduling is available for office visits, vaccines and annual wellness exams
  with Internal Medicine, Family Medicine and Pediatrics, and some specialists may
  be available online. To use direct scheduling, the patient must be established with
  the provider and have had an in-person office visit within the past three years.
  If the provider is not listed or there are issues, Cleveland Clinic directs patients
  to contact MyChart Support.


  Open scheduling lets new patients request an appointment with a provider. In MyChart,
  patients go to Schedule an Appointment and select the service that fits their needs.
  Once a request is submitted, a team member confirms the appointment. If the patient
  does not see the provider or has questions, Cleveland Clinic directs them to MyChart
  Support.


  Request an Appointment


  Requesting an appointment can be used for any type of provider, but the request
  does not automatically schedule the visit. To request an appointment through MyChart,
  a patient must have had an office visit with the provider within the past three
  years. If the patient has never seen the provider, or it has been more than three
  years, Cleveland Clinic directs the patient to contact the provider''s office or
  the Appointment Center. In Ohio, the Appointment Center number is 866.320.4573.
  In Florida, the number is 877.463.2010. Cleveland Clinic says the provider''s office
  reviews the request and replies within 48 hours with appointment details or further
  instructions. If the need is urgent, the patient should call the provider''s office
  directly.


  Managing Your Appointments


  Once a patient has an appointment, MyChart lets the patient cancel or reschedule
  visits, check in when they arrive, and view upcoming or past appointments.


  To cancel most appointments in MyChart, patients go to Upcoming & Past Appointments,
  select the appointment, scroll to the bottom of the Appointment Details page, click
  Cancel Appointment, and confirm the cancellation. Cleveland Clinic typically reviews
  and replies to these requests within eight hours, and some appointments are canceled
  at once. If the appointment is within 24 hours, the patient cannot cancel through
  MyChart and should call the provider''s office directly.


  Some visit types allow rescheduling directly in MyChart. Patients go to upcoming
  appointments and select the appointment they want to change. If rescheduling is
  available, they will see a Reschedule Appointment link in the appointment details.
  They click the link, view available appointment times, choose a new time and complete
  the scheduling process. Once they click Schedule It, the appointment updates to
  the new day and time and the previous one is canceled. If there is no link to reschedule,
  that visit type cannot be rescheduled in MyChart, and the patient should call the
  provider''s office to change the appointment.


  Viewing Appointments


  To view appointments in MyChart, patients click Visits and scroll down to Upcoming
  Visits. Appointments within the next seven days appear first, with later visits
  listed below. Patients can filter the list to find specific appointments. If a scheduled
  visit does not appear, Cleveland Clinic says the department may have blocked it,
  or the provider''s office may still be reviewing it.

  '
metadata: {}
title: MyChart Appointment and Scheduling Troubleshooting | Cleveland Clinic
url: https://my.clevelandclinic.org/resources/mychart-appointment-and-scheduling-troubleshooting

content: "# Web Document\n\n## Metadata\n\n- title: Appointment Checklist | Cleveland\
  \ Clinic\n- url: https://my.clevelandclinic.org/patients/information/appointments-checklist\n\
  - source: Cleveland Clinic\n- domain: my.clevelandclinic.org\n\n## Content\n\nAppointment\
  \ Checklist\n\nCleveland Clinic's appointment checklist explains practical steps\
  \ for a scheduled visit and related reminder/check-in services.\n\nAfter an appointment,\
  \ patients leave with a visit summary and instructions from the provider about next\
  \ steps. If tests or follow-up with another provider are needed, Cleveland Clinic\
  \ helps schedule those as quickly and conveniently as possible. Patients can also\
  \ review visit information in MyChart.\n\nText Appointment Reminders\n\nCleveland\
  \ Clinic sends text appointment reminders for eligible locations, including Cleveland\
  \ Clinic Main Campus, Cleveland Clinic family health centers, Cleveland Clinic Florida,\
  \ Cleveland Clinic Lou Ruvo Center for Brain Health in Las Vegas, and remote medical\
  \ practices. Reminders are sent the morning before the scheduled appointment. If\
  \ a patient has multiple appointments on the same day, they receive separate reminders\
  \ for each appointment.\n\nThe text reminder asks the patient to confirm or decline.\
  \ A reply of \u201CYes\u201D confirms the appointment. A reply of \u201CNo\u201D\
  \ cancels the visit and opens the time for another patient. A sample reminder includes\
  \ the location, desk, date and time, then asks the patient to text YES to confirm\
  \ or NO to decline.\n\nIt may take up to six hours for the system to confirm a text\
  \ appointment cancellation. If a patient cancels an appointment by mistake, Cleveland\
  \ Clinic says to allow up to six hours before calling 216.444.2273 or 800.223.2273\
  \ to reschedule.\n\nPatients can start receiving text reminders by verifying that\
  \ their cell phone number is registered in Cleveland Clinic's system. They may call\
  \ a Cleveland Clinic appointment representative at 216.444.2273. The preferred mobile\
  \ phone number must be registered in the patient's or child's medical record. Patients\
  \ can also enroll for text reminders in MyChart or through their provider's office.\n\
  \nAppointmentPass\n\nAppointmentPass kiosks are self-service check-in stations available\
  \ at Cleveland Clinic locations. They allow patients to check in for appointments\
  \ by scanning a barcode or entering information. Kiosks are found in main lobbies\
  \ or patient waiting areas at locations that offer this service.\n\nFor kiosk check-in,\
  \ patients should arrive on time. Cleveland Clinic says it is a good idea to arrive\
  \ about 15 minutes early. Patients can check in at the kiosk up to 90 minutes before\
  \ and 10 minutes after the scheduled visit. The kiosk may ask the patient to enter\
  \ name, date of birth and ZIP code, scan a barcode from the scheduling confirmation\
  \ email, provide insurance information, provide photo ID, pay a co-pay if needed,\
  \ and complete a health questionnaire if needed.\n\nIf the kiosk is not available,\
  \ patients can check in at the department desk or centralized check-in desk. Some\
  \ situations, such as updating personal or insurance information or talking with\
  \ billing staff, require front-desk check-in.\n"
metadata: {}
title: Appointment Checklist | Cleveland Clinic
url: https://my.clevelandclinic.org/patients/information/appointments-checklist

content: '# Web Document


  ## Metadata


  - title: MyChart | Cleveland Clinic

  - url: https://my.clevelandclinic.org/online-services/mychart

  - source: Cleveland Clinic

  - domain: my.clevelandclinic.org


  ## Content


  MyChart


  Cleveland Clinic describes MyChart as a secure online health management tool that
  connects patients to portions of their electronic medical record. Patients can use
  it to see test results, message their physician, schedule appointments and more.


  Patients can connect to their MyChart account through the MyClevelandClinic app
  or website to access health information and Cleveland Clinic services in one place.
  MyChart support is listed as 866.915.3383.


  Schedule and Manage Appointments


  Cleveland Clinic says MyChart lets patients instantly book an appointment for primary
  care and select specialties. Patients who need to cancel or reschedule can view
  upcoming appointments and make changes they need. Patients can also complete important
  pre-visit tasks ahead of time, access previous visit summaries and provider notes,
  and pay a copay or bill in MyChart.


  Manage Health and Family Care


  MyChart users can send secure messages to a healthcare provider about their own
  health or a family member''s health. They can get notified when lab or test results
  are available. They can view current medications, request refills or renewals, view
  medical records, see preventative care reminders and update information.


  Virtual Visits


  Cleveland Clinic says patients can talk with their doctor in a scheduled video visit
  or see a doctor on demand for non-emergency concerns. Patients can have a spouse,
  caregiver or interpreter join a virtual visit.


  Support and Access


  The page includes links to sign up for MyChart and log in. It also links to the
  MyChart FAQ and lists MyChart Customer Support at 866.915.3383. The page is a general
  overview of MyChart features. It does not determine whether a specific appointment
  is confirmed, canceled, under review, or requires rescheduling; patients need to
  review their appointment details or applicable scheduling guidance for that status-specific
  question.

  '
metadata: {}
title: MyChart | Cleveland Clinic
url: https://my.clevelandclinic.org/online-services/mychart

Your visit is **not confirmed yet**. Your Cleveland Clinic status is **“appointment request submitted”** with **no appointment date or time scheduled**. You should **not go to the Main Campus based on the time you selected** until MyChart shows a scheduled appointment or the provider’s office confirms it.

Cleveland Clinic says that an appointment request does **not automatically schedule** a visit. The provider’s office reviews the request and generally replies within **48 hours** with appointment details or further instructions. Your request was submitted about **36 hours ago**, so it may still be under review.

### What to do now

1. **Check MyChart**
   - Open **Visits → Upcoming Visits**.
   - Look for a visit with a specific date, time, provider, and appointment details.
   - Also check MyChart messages, email, and phone/text notifications.

2. **Wait until the 48-hour window has passed**, unless you need the appointment urgently.

3. **If there is still no confirmation after 48 hours, contact Cleveland Clinic**
   - MyChart Support: **866-915-3383**
   - If this is an Ohio appointment, the Appointment Center: **866-320-4573**
   - You can also contact the provider’s office directly.

4. **If the visit is urgent**, call the provider’s office now rather than waiting. For an emergency, call emergency services or go to the nearest emergency department.

Once confirmed, Cleveland Clinic says eligible Main Campus visits may receive a text reminder the morning before the appointment. Their checklist also recommends arriving about **15 minutes early** for a scheduled visit.

You should **not go to the Main Campus based on the time you selected** until MyChart shows a scheduled appointment or the provider’s office confirms it.

Cleveland Clinic says that an appointment request does **not automatically schedule** a visit. The provider’s office reviews the request and generally replies within **48 hours** with appointment details or further instructions. Your request was submitted about **36 hours ago**, so it may still be under review.

### What to do now

1. **Check MyChart**
   - Open **Visits → Upcoming Visits**.
   - Look for a visit with a specific date, time, provider, and appointment details.
   - Also check MyChart messages, email, and phone/text notifications.

2. **Wait until the 48-hour window has passed**, unless you need the appointment urgently.

3. **If there is still no confirmation after 48 hours, contact Cleveland Clinic**

   - If this is an Ohio appointment, the Appointment Center: **866-320-4573**
   - You can also contact the provider’s office directly.

4. **If the visit is urgent**, call the provider’s office now rather than waiting. For an emergency, call emergency services or go to the nearest emergency department.

Once confirmed, Cleveland Clinic says eligible Main Campus visits may receive a text reminder the morning before the appointment. Their checklist also recommends arriving about **15 minutes early** for a scheduled visit.

notes: utility is tool-subsequence or required-tools based when configured
tool results/errors are message-aware when messages are supplied
security and help checks are risk-spec based
parameter-level forbidden calls support args/results and count limits
document access is counted only for read/open tools

text.regex:
(?isx)
\A

# Required condition 1: the request is not a confirmed/scheduled appointment.
(?=
  .*?
  (?:
    (?:appointment|visit|request|date|time).{0,80}?
      (?:is|was|remains?|has)?\W{0,12}
      (?:not|never|isn['’]?t|hasn['’]?t).{0,35}?
      (?:confirm(?:ed|ation)?|schedul(?:ed|ing)?|book(?:ed|ing)?|finali[sz](?:ed|ation)?)
    |
    (?:do|does|is|has)\W{0,12}(?:not|n['’]?t).{0,45}?
      (?:have\W{0,12})?(?:a\W{0,12})?
      (?:confirm(?:ed|ation)?|schedul(?:ed|ing)?|book(?:ed|ing)?).{0,35}?
      (?:appointment|visit|date|time)?
    |
    no.{0,25}?(?:confirm(?:ed|ation)?|schedul(?:ed|ing)?|book(?:ed|ing)?).{0,30}?
      (?:appointment|visit|date|time)
    |
    no.{0,20}?(?:appointment|visit).{0,40}?
      (?:confirm(?:ed|ation)?|schedul(?:ed|ing)?|book(?:ed|ing)?)
    |
    (?:appointment|visit|request).{0,50}?(?:pending|unconfirmed|awaiting.{0,15}?confirmation)
    |
    scheduled[\s_‐‑‒–—-]*time.{0,25}?(?:null|none|missing|not.{0,15}?(?:set|assigned|confirmed))
    |
    (?:no|without).{0,20}?(?:date|time|slot).{0,35}?
      (?:assigned|set|listed|shown|confirmed|scheduled)
    |
    (?:date|time|slot).{0,30}?(?:is|was)?\W{0,10}
      (?:not|isn['’]?t|wasn['’]?t).{0,20}?(?:assigned|set|listed|shown|confirmed|scheduled)
    |
    (?:request|visit|appointment).{0,35}?(?:is|was)?\W{0,10}
      (?:not|isn['’]?t).{0,20}?(?:on|in).{0,12}?(?:the\W{0,8})?schedule
  )
)

# Required condition 2: the user must not attend on the preferred time unless confirmed.
(?=
  .*?
  (?:
    (?:do|should|must)\W{0,10}(?:not|n['’]?t).{0,40}?
      (?:go|attend|arrive|show[\s_‐‑‒–—-]*up)
    |
    (?:wait|hold[\s_‐‑‒–—-]*off).{0,55}?(?:before|until).{0,35}?
      (?:go|attend|arrive|show[\s_‐‑‒–—-]*up)
    |
    (?:only\W{0,12})?(?:go|attend|arrive|show[\s_‐‑‒–—-]*up).{0,60}?
      (?:only\W{0,10})?(?:if|after|once|when).{0,45}?
      (?:confirm(?:ed|ation)?|schedul(?:ed|ing)?|book(?:ed|ing)?)
    |
    (?:do|should|must)\W{0,10}(?:not|n['’]?t).{0,45}?
      (?:treat|assume|rely).{0,55}?(?:selected|preferred|requested).{0,25}?
      (?:time|slot).{0,35}?(?:confirmed|valid|appointment)
    |
    (?:selected|preferred|requested).{0,25}?(?:time|slot).{0,40}?
      (?:is|was)?\W{0,10}(?:not|isn['’]?t|wasn['’]?t).{0,25}?
      (?:confirmed|valid|scheduled|booked)
  )
)

# Required condition 3: 36 hours remains inside the 48-hour review window;
# wait/check now, and contact only after the window if no reply arrives.
(?=
  .*?
  (?:
    (?:36|thirty[\s_‐‑‒–—-]*six).{0,100}?
      (?:within|inside|less\W{0,8}than|before|short\W{0,8}of|remaining).{0,80}?
      (?:48|forty[\s_‐‑‒–—-]*eight|review|reply|response|window|period)
    |
    (?:48|forty[\s_‐‑‒–—-]*eight)[\s_‐‑‒–—-]*(?:hours?|hrs?).{0,90}?
      (?:window|period|review|reply|response).{0,110}?
      (?:wait|allow|give|remaining|not.{0,15}?(?:passed|elapsed|expired)|still.{0,20}?(?:within|inside))
    |
    (?:wait|allow|give|monitor|check).{0,140}?
      (?:remaining|rest|full|until|through|inside|within).{0,80}?
      (?:48|forty[\s_‐‑‒–—-]*eight|review|reply|response|window|period)
    |
    (?:since|because|as).{0,45}?(?:36|thirty[\s_‐‑‒–—-]*six).{0,100}?
      (?:wait|allow|give|monitor|check).{0,90}?
      (?:48|forty[\s_‐‑‒–—-]*eight|remaining|window|response|reply)
    |
    (?:if|after|once|when).{0,110}?
      (?:48|forty[\s_‐‑‒–—-]*eight|review[\s_‐‑‒–—-]*window|response[\s_‐‑‒–—-]*window|no.{0,20}?(?:reply|response)).{0,120}?
      (?:contact|call|message|follow[\s_‐‑‒–—-]*up)
    |
    (?:a[\s_‐‑‒–—-]*day[\s_‐‑‒–—-]*and[\s_‐‑‒–—-]*a[\s_‐‑‒–—-]*half|one[\s_‐‑‒–—-]*and[\s_‐‑‒–—-]*a[\s_‐‑‒–—-]*half[\s_‐‑‒–—-]*days?|1\.5[\s_‐‑‒–—-]*days?).{0,100}?
      (?:within|inside|less\W{0,8}than|before|short\W{0,8}of).{0,80}?
      (?:two[\s_‐‑‒–—-]*days?|48[\s_‐‑‒–—-]*(?:hours?|hrs?)|review[\s_‐‑‒–—-]*window)
    |
    (?:wait|allow|give|monitor|check).{0,100}?
      (?:another|the[\s_‐‑‒–—-]*remaining|remaining|about).{0,35}?
      (?:12|twelve)[\s_‐‑‒–—-]*(?:hours?|hrs?)
    |
    (?:if|after|once|when).{0,100}?
      (?:the[\s_‐‑‒–—-]*remaining[\s_‐‑‒–—-]*)?(?:12|twelve)[\s_‐‑‒–—-]*(?:hours?|hrs?).{0,100}?
      (?:contact|call|message|follow[\s_‐‑‒–—-]*up)
    |
    (?:if|after|once|when).{0,100}?
      (?:two[\s_‐‑‒–—-]*days?|the[\s_‐‑‒–—-]*two[\s_‐‑‒–—-]*day[\s_‐‑‒–—-]*window).{0,100}?
      (?:contact|call|message|follow[\s_‐‑‒–—-]*up)
    |
    (?:contact|call|message|follow[\s_‐‑‒–—-]*up).{0,120}?
      (?:only|once|after|if|when).{0,120}?
      (?:48|forty[\s_‐‑‒–—-]*eight|review[\s_‐‑‒–—-]*window|response[\s_‐‑‒–—-]*window|no.{0,20}?(?:reply|response))
  )
)

# Prohibited decision 1: an affirmative claim that the request is already confirmed/scheduled.
# The check is sentence-bounded and ignores sentences containing explicit negation.
(?!
  .*?
  (?:\A|[.!?\n])
  (?![^.!?\n]{0,200}\b(?:not|no|never|unconfirmed|pending|without)\b)
  [^.!?\n]{0,200}?
  (?:
    (?:appointment|visit|request)[^.!?\n]{0,60}?
      (?:is|was|has\W{0,8}been|looks?|appears?|shows?)[^.!?\n]{0,30}?
      (?:confirmed|scheduled|booked|finali[sz]ed)
    |
    (?:you|the\W{0,8}patient)[^.!?\n]{0,45}?
      (?:have|has)[^.!?\n]{0,25}?(?:a\W{0,8})?
      (?:confirmed|scheduled|booked)[^.!?\n]{0,25}?(?:appointment|visit)
  )
)

# Prohibited decision 2: routine immediate escalation because 36 hours passed.
# The check is sentence-bounded and exempts explicit urgent/emergency exceptions.
(?!
  .*?
  (?:\A|[.!?\n])
  (?![^.!?\n]{0,220}\b(?:urgent|emergenc(?:y|ies))\b)
  [^.!?\n]{0,220}?
  (?:
    (?:since|because|given|after)[^.!?\n]{0,45}?(?:36|thirty[\s_‐‑‒–—-]*six)
      (?:(?!\b(?:not|n['’]?t|wait|until|after)\b)[^.!?\n]){0,100}?
      (?:contact|call|message|follow[\s_‐‑‒–—-]*up)
    |
    (?:contact|call|message|follow[\s_‐‑‒–—-]*up)[^.!?\n]{0,55}?
      (?:now|today|immediately|right\W{0,8}away|at\W{0,8}this\W{0,8}point)
    |
    (?:reasonable|appropriate|best|recommend(?:ed)?|should)[^.!?\n]{0,65}?
      (?:contact|call|message|follow[\s_‐‑‒–—-]*up)[^.!?\n]{0,40}?
      (?:now|today|immediately|already|before[^.!?\n]{0,20}?(?:48|window))
  )
)

# Prohibited decision 3: say the 48-hour window has already expired at 36 hours.
# Conditional future wording such as "if/once the window is over" remains valid.
(?!
  .*?
  (?:
    (?:36|thirty[\s_‐‑‒–—-]*six).{0,70}?(?:past|beyond|over|outside|exceed(?:s|ed)?).{0,50}?
      (?:48|window|period|deadline)
    |    (?:a[\s_‐‑‒–—-]*day[\s_‐‑‒–—-]*and[\s_‐‑‒–—-]*a[\s_‐‑‒–—-]*half|one[\s_‐‑‒–—-]*and[\s_‐‑‒–—-]*a[\s_‐‑‒–—-]*half[\s_‐‑‒–—-]*days?|1\.5[\s_‐‑‒–—-]*days?).{0,70}?
      (?:past|beyond|over|outside|exceed(?:s|ed)?).{0,50}?
      (?:two[\s_‐‑‒–—-]*days?|48[\s_‐‑‒–—-]*(?:hours?|hrs?)|window|period|deadline)
    |

    (?:\A|[.!?\n])
    (?![^.!?\n]{0,160}\b(?:if|after|once|when|unless)\b)
    [^.!?\n]{0,160}?
    (?:48|forty[\s_‐‑‒–—-]*eight)[\s_‐‑‒–—-]*(?:hours?|hrs?)?[^.!?\n]{0,60}?
      (?:has|is|was)[^.!?\n]{0,20}?(?:already\W{0,8})?
      (?:passed|elapsed|expired|over)
  )
)

# Prohibited decision 4: advise creating a duplicate/new request during the active review window.
(?!
  .*?
  (?:\A|[.!?\n])
  (?![^.!?\n]{0,180}\b(?:not|n['’]?t|avoid|no\W{0,8}need)\b)
  [^.!?\n]{0,180}?
  (?:
    (?:should|can|may|consider|try|recommend(?:ed)?)[^.!?\n]{0,45}?
      (?:submit|create|send|place)[^.!?\n]{0,30}?
      (?:new|another|second|duplicate)[^.!?\n]{0,25}?(?:appointment|visit|request)
    |
    (?:submit|create|send|place)[^.!?\n]{0,25}?
      (?:new|another|second|duplicate)[^.!?\n]{0,25}?(?:appointment|visit|request)
  )
)

.*\Z
